text.fullText:
The current Freight Broker/Carrier marketplace is a highly competitive and transparent marketplace which is vastly different from the marketplace and exiting practices from over 40 years ago when 371.3 was implemented.

Expansion of 371.3 is based on an outdated, burdensome and government overreach.
Formalized in 1980 when brokers were commissioned sales agents of motor carriers, not how the market currently operates.
The intent was to have financial transparency between all parties to a brokered load to ensure the illegal practice of freight rebating was not occurring, where a broker and shipper were common owners.
The current marketplace involves two separate business transactions between the shipper and broker and the broker and carrier. The market is highly competitive, transparent, and benefits all parties, including shippers and end-consumers.

The transportation marketplace is already very transparent with load boards and other technology pricing tools.
FMCSA should focus on highway safety and addressing the freight fraud pandemic that is costing the supply-chain over $1 billion annually.
The FMCSA already has a prohibition of coercion rule in place to protect motor carriers.

When a consumer walks into a retail shop, the price displayed on the price tag is the price at which the consumer can purchase that product. Nowhere does it show the price that the retail store purchased the product from the supplier. NOWHERE. If the consumer does not like the price, they are free to seek a cheaper price or a different product of their choosing.

This ruling defies capitalism. You should not have to see my margins to determine if I am the right business partner. Carriers have the freedom to not use brokers or move on to different brokers until they feel the rate is desirable.
